frame_analyses.description: Setup view showing blue laparoscopic trainer with slit Penrose drain and suture material. Two black marks visible on drain surface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS trainer setup with proper drain positioning

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle at approximately 2/3 point from tip, positioning for suture placement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp position as indicated by instructional text overlay

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver positioning suture through first mark on drain. Instructional overlay shows proper technique for grabbing suture behind needle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Following proper technique with suture manipulation behind needle to reduce tissue damage

frame_analyses.description: Using dissector to pivot suture and create short tag as instructed. Suture appears to be passing through drain tissue.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good use of dissector for suture manipulation

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot tying sequence. Instructional overlay shows C-shape formation for first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Starting surgeon's knot formation with proper C-shape technique

frame_analyses.description: Placing dissector on top of C-shape and rotating to create hump in suture for easier knot formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good technique creating suture hump to facilitate knot tying

frame_analyses.description: Making reversed C with left hand, rotating externally and placing needle holder on top of C for second throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching technique demonstrated between throws

frame_analyses.description: Completing final knot sequence with both instruments working to secure the knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bilateral instrument coordination for knot completion

frame_analyses.description: Final view showing completed suture placement with knot secured on drain surface
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task appears completed with suture in place

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw formation using C-shape technique
Proper hand switching demonstrated, reversed C technique
Final throw with appropriate hand switching

confidence_rationale: This appears to be an instructional video demonstrating proper technique rather than a performance assessment. Suture placement accuracy difficult to assess due to camera angles and instructional overlays. No clear penalties visible but precision measurements limited.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation measurements due to instructional video format and camera angles
Whether this represents actual task performance or demonstration
Precise timing due to instructional pauses and overlays
technique_summary: Excellent demonstration of proper intracorporeal suturing technique with appropriate hand switching, good instrument handling, and systematic knot formation. Performance appears to be intermediate level with good technical execution.
strengths: Correct hand switching between all throws
Good use of dissector for suture manipulation
improvement_suggestions: This appears to be an instructional demonstration rather than a performance requiring improvement
